title: Fix blank login page and Internet Identity authentication

requirements.text: Fix the LoginPage so it renders correctly on initial load without showing a blank screen. Ensure the Internet Identity authentication flow initializes properly, handles the AuthClient loading state, and displays the login UI (app name, tagline, and 'Login with Internet Identity' button) before any authentication check completes.

quotes: it is blank the login page is not working i guess fix the problems

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the root URL '/' displays the TrackFlow login page with a visible 'Login with Internet Identity' button instead of a blank screen
The login page does not remain blank while AuthClient is initializing — a loading spinner or the full login UI is shown immediately
Clicking 'Login with Internet Identity' triggers the Internet Identity popup correctly
After successful authentication, the user is redirected to the pricing page (/pricing)
If the user is already authenticated on page load, they are automatically redirected to /dashboard without seeing the login page

requirements.text: Audit and fix the App.tsx route tree and auth guard logic to ensure that unauthenticated users are correctly redirected to the login page and authenticated users are not blocked or redirected back to login. Fix any router initialization issues that may cause the entire app to render blank (e.g., missing router provider, incorrect route configuration, or premature auth checks before identity is resolved).

quotes: it is blank the login page is not working i guess fix the problems

acceptanceCriteria: The app never renders a completely blank white page on any route
Unauthenticated users visiting any protected route (e.g., /dashboard) are redirected to /login
Authenticated users visiting /login or / are redirected to /dashboard
The auth guard waits for Internet Identity initialization before performing redirects, preventing redirect loops or flashes of blank content

requirements.text: Ensure the useInternetIdentity hook and useActor hook are correctly wired in the component tree so that the identity state is available to LoginPage and all protected pages on initial mount. Fix any context provider ordering issues in main.tsx that might prevent the identity from being accessible.

quotes: it is blank the login page is not working i guess fix the problems

acceptanceCriteria: useInternetIdentity() returns a valid identity state (isAuthenticated, login, logout) on the LoginPage without throwing context errors
No 'Cannot read properties of undefined' or missing context errors appear in the browser console on initial load
The QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider correctly wrap the router/app so all pages have access to both contexts
The backend actor is created successfully after login and queries to the backend (e.g., getUserProfile) work without actor initialization errors

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Preserve all existing route paths and page components — only fix initialization and rendering issues
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or layout of the login page
Modifying backend logic or data models
Changing plan pricing or payment flow
